headline: Federal land administration has 340,000 unresolved title disputes
context: The Department of Director General of Lands and Mines reported 340,000 unresolved land title disputes nationwide in 2025, some dating back to the 1970s.

cards.big: 340,000 land title disputes unresolved — some families have waited since the 1970s
cards.sub: Half a century of bureaucratic failure trapping Malaysians in property limbo

cards.big: National Land Code Section 340 provides 'indefeasibility of title' — in theory
cards.sub: Reality is 340,000 cases where this legal protection has failed or stalled

cards.big: Unresolved titles lock an estimated RM120 billion in property value out of the economy
cards.sub: Disputed land cannot be sold, developed, mortgaged, or productively used

cards.big: Land offices have 4,200 staff processing 340,000 disputes — 81 cases per officer
cards.sub: Systematic understaffing means the backlog grows faster than it can be resolved

cards.big: 340,000 families in limbo means 340,000 families who can't fully participate in the economy
Should a special land title tribunal with expedited procedures be created to clear the backlog?
